core_model.structure_summary: Second-order phase oscillators with inertia and linear damping, each driven by a constant term and coupled to graph neighbors through weighted sines of pairwise phase differences, with heterogeneous heavy-tailed weights and studied via order parameters measuring collective coherence.
core_model.canonical_form: \ddot{x}_i + a\,\dot{x}_i = c_i + K \sum_{j=1}^{N} W_{ij} \sin(x_j - x_i), \qquad z(t) = \frac{1}{N}\sum_j e^{i x_j(t)}

variables.meaning: phase angle of each unit on the graph
linear damping coefficient on the first derivative
constant per-unit driving term (heterogeneous natural drift)
weighted adjacency matrix with heavy-tailed (power-law) weight distribution
global coupling strength controlling the coherence transition
modulus of the mean complex exponential of phases, quantifying collective coherence

notes: The paper combines (a) the second-order phase-coupled oscillator system on empirical heterogeneous graphs and (b) empirical statistical analysis of increment fluctuations via superstatistics (mixture of Gaussians over an inverse-variance parameter, yielding q-Gaussians). The dynamical core the main synchronization results are about is (a); the superstatistics part is a data-analysis tool, noted here as a secondary candidate. Coupling weights and driving terms follow heavy-tailed distributions, which is where the scale_invariance tag applies.
